events.change_summary: Intent check: fix --url stream by reading actual URL from webtorrent stdout

events.summary: Intent check pushed: fix --url by reading the real stream URL from webtorrent's stdout instead of polling a hardcoded path that no longer works. URL shortening is explicitly out of scope for now.

events.change_summary: Full scope: wire _find_stream_url into --url mode, remove HTTP polling loop, update tests S13–S19

events.rationale: Bug fix: restores broken --url behavior by wiring an existing function into the launch path. No UI changes (skip design). Well-contained to 2 files using an already-written helper (skip architecture).

events.decision: Use existing _find_stream_url function unchanged — wire it in, don't rewrite it
events.rationale: The function already implements the correct approach (daemon thread + queue + port-anchored regex) matching prior project decisions. The only gap is that it was never called in main(). Introducing new URL-scanning logic would duplicate code and risk diverging from the established pattern.

events.decision: Remove --quiet from the webtorrent command and pipe stdout
events.rationale: --quiet suppresses stdout entirely, which is why the existing _find_stream_url function never received the URL announcement. Piping stdout (stdout=subprocess.PIPE, text=True) makes the URL readable. The two changes are inseparable.

events.summary: Full scope (revision 2) pushed. Single phase: remove the 120-second urlopen polling loop, add stdout=PIPE + text=True to Popen, remove --quiet, call the already-written _find_stream_url to get the URL from webtorrent's stdout, substitute LAN IP for localhost. Tests S13–S19 rewritten to mock proc.stdout instead of urlopen; S13.test_url_flag_popen_not_piped replaced with a --quiet absence check; new test for timeout/empty-stdout path. Workflow: bug-fix, standard tier, scope → implementation → review → release.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.note: Beginning Phase 1: replace HTTP polling with stdout URL scanning in dhtplay and rewrite S13-S19 tests

events.description: Starting phase 1: Replace HTTP polling with stdout URL scanning
Implementing core changes: rewriting --url block and updating tests

events.details: 44/44 passed. S13 gained one new test (test_url_flag_timeout_exits_nonzero_no_stdout); test_url_flag_popen_not_piped replaced by test_url_flag_no_quiet; test_url_flag_prints_http_url_with_infohash replaced by test_url_flag_prints_http_url_with_lan_ip. All S13-S19 urlopen mocks removed and proc_mock.stdout iterators added.

events.decision: Implementation: patched _find_stream_url in timeout test rather than using iter([]) with the real function
events.rationale: iter([]) with the real _find_stream_url would block for the full 30s timeout in tests. Per project memory: any test exercising a q.get(timeout=N) path must either provide real data or patch the function. The timeout test patches _find_stream_url to return None directly, keeping test runtime under 1s.

events.change_summary: Phase 1 complete: Replace HTTP polling with stdout URL scanning

events.summary: Replaced 120-second urlopen polling loop with _find_stream_url stdout scanning. Removed --quiet and added stdout=PIPE+text=True to Popen. LAN IP substituted via re.sub. Tests S13-S19 rewritten to use proc_mock.stdout iterators; timeout test patches _find_stream_url to avoid 30s wait. 44/44 passing.

items.description: Run `python3 test_dhtplay.py` and confirm output ends with `44/44 passed  — ALL PASS`
Run `grep -n 'urlopen' dhtplay` and confirm no output (the HTTP polling loop is fully removed)
Run `grep -n '_find_stream_url' dhtplay` and confirm exactly two lines: the function definition and one call site inside the `if args.url:` block
Run `grep -n '\-\-quiet' dhtplay` and confirm no output
Run `./dhtplay <any-40-hex-infohash> --url --dry-run` — should print the magnet URI and exit 0 (dry-run bypasses the url branch entirely, verifying the non-url path is unaffected)
With webtorrent-cli installed, run `./dhtplay <infohash> --url` and confirm: a URL containing your LAN IP is printed within ~30 seconds of webtorrent finding metadata, not after a 2-minute wait
Confirm the printed URL does not contain `localhost` or `127.0.0.1` (unless no LAN IP is available)

stageContent.review:
## Verdict

**Verdict:** APPROVE
**Files reviewed:** 2 files changed across 1 phases

All 7 acceptance criteria pass. The HTTP polling loop is fully removed, the --url branch correctly pipes webtorrent stdout and calls _find_stream_url, LAN IP substitution is applied, and the timeout path exits non-zero with no stdout output. 44/44 tests pass.

### Automated Checks

| Check | Result | Details |
|-------|--------|---------|
| Test suite | PASS | 44/44 passed — ALL PASS |

### Acceptance Criteria (7/7 passed)

- [x] AC-1: `dhtplay <infohash> --url` exits 0 and prints a line containing `http://<lan-ip>:8888/` when webtorrent announces a URL on stdout; verified by S13 passing — PASS: S13_UrlFlag.test_url_flag_prints_http_url_with_lan_ip passes. dhtplay:218-258: Popen launched with stdout=PIPE+text=True, _find_stream_url reads URL, re.sub replaces host, print outputs the URL, returns 0.
- [x] AC-2: The printed URL contains the LAN IP from `get_lan_ip()`, not `localhost` or `127.0.0.1`; verified by `S13.test_url_flag_prints_http_url_with_lan_ip` — PASS: dhtplay:235: re.sub(r'https?://(?:localhost|127\.0\.0\.1)', f'http://{lan_ip}', raw_url). Test mocks get_lan_ip returning '192.168.1.100' and asserts '192.168.1.100' in output and 'localhost' not in output — passes.
- [x] AC-3: When `proc.stdout` yields no URL-matching line, the command exits non-zero and stdout is empty; verified by the new timeout test (R8) — PASS: S13_UrlFlag.test_url_flag_timeout_exits_nonzero_no_stdout passes. dhtplay:228-233: when _find_stream_url returns None, prints error to stderr and returns 3. Test patches _find_stream_url to return None, asserts rc != 0 and out.getvalue() == ''.
- [x] AC-4: The `--url` Popen call does not include `--quiet` in its argument list; verified by the replacement S13 test (R7) — PASS: S13_UrlFlag.test_url_flag_no_quiet passes. dhtplay:219: wt_cmd_http = [wt, magnet, '-p', str(args.port), '--keep-seeding'] — no --quiet present. Test captures all Popen cmd args and asserts '--quiet' not in captured_args.
- [x] AC-5: `_find_stream_url` is called with the subprocess handle and `args.port`; no new URL-scanning logic is introduced; verified by reading `dhtplay` lines 221–270 after the change — PASS: dhtplay:227: raw_url = _find_stream_url(proc, args.port, timeout=30). grep shows exactly two lines: line 116 (definition) and line 227 (call site inside if args.url: block). No new URL-scanning logic introduced.
- [x] AC-6: `grep -n "urlopen" dhtplay` returns no results — the HTTP polling loop is fully removed — PASS: grep -n 'urlopen' dhtplay returns exit code 1 with no output. The polling loop and urllib imports are fully removed.
- [x] AC-7: `python3 test_dhtplay.py` exits 0 with all scenarios passing after the change — PASS: python3 test_dhtplay.py: 44/44 passed — ALL PASS. Exit code 0.

### Code Quality (Refactor Review)

No code quality issues found in changed files.

### Security Assessment (Security Review)

No security issues found in changed files.

### Decisions Made During Implementation

- Use existing _find_stream_url function unchanged — wire it in, don't rewrite it
- Remove --quiet from the webtorrent command and pipe stdout so the URL announcement reaches _find_stream_url
- Patch _find_stream_url in timeout test rather than using iter([]) with the real function to avoid blocking for the full 30s timeout

## Required Changes

None.
stageContent.implementation:
## Phase 1: Replace HTTP polling with stdout URL scanning

### Changes

**`dhtplay`**
- Removed unused imports: `import time`, `import urllib.request`, `import urllib.parse`
- Rewrote `if args.url:` block: removed `import tempfile`, `--quiet`, `--out out_dir`, `--keep-seeding` stays, added `stdout=subprocess.PIPE, text=True` to Popen
- Removed the 120-second `urlopen` polling loop entirely
- Added `_find_stream_url(proc, args.port, timeout=30)` call; handles `None` return with stderr message and exit 3
- Added `re.sub(r'https?://(?:localhost|127\.0\.0\.1)', f'http://{lan_ip}', raw_url)` for LAN IP substitution

**`test_dhtplay.py`**
- S13: renamed `test_url_flag_prints_http_url_with_infohash` → `test_url_flag_prints_http_url_with_lan_ip`; removed urlopen mock; added `proc_mock.stdout = iter([...])`
- S13: updated `test_url_flag_no_vlc` — same mock pattern change
- S13: replaced `test_url_flag_popen_not_piped` with `test_url_flag_no_quiet` (verifies `--quiet` absent from Popen args)
- S13: added `test_url_flag_timeout_exits_nonzero_no_stdout` (patches `_find_stream_url` to return `None`; verifies exit non-zero, stdout empty)
- S14, S17, S18, S19: removed urlopen mocks; added `proc_mock.stdout = iter(["http://localhost:8888/Movie.mkv\n"])`
- S17: updated `expected_url` from infohash-based path to `http://192.168.1.100:8888/Movie.mkv`

### Result
44/44 tests passing (up from 43 — one new test added). All verification greps clean.

reviewPayload.summary: All 7 acceptance criteria pass. The HTTP polling loop is fully removed, the --url branch correctly pipes webtorrent stdout and calls _find_stream_url, LAN IP substitution is applied, and the timeout path exits non-zero with no stdout output. 44/44 tests pass.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `dhtplay <infohash> --url` exits 0 and prints a line containing `http://<lan-ip>:8888/` when webtorrent announces a URL on stdout; verified by S13 passing
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: S13_UrlFlag.test_url_flag_prints_http_url_with_lan_ip passes. dhtplay:218-258: Popen launched with stdout=PIPE+text=True, _find_stream_url reads URL, re.sub replaces host, print outputs the URL, returns 0.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The printed URL contains the LAN IP from `get_lan_ip()`, not `localhost` or `127.0.0.1`; verified by `S13.test_url_flag_prints_http_url_with_lan_ip`
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: dhtplay:235: re.sub(r'https?://(?:localhost|127\.0\.0\.1)', f'http://{lan_ip}', raw_url). Test mocks get_lan_ip returning '192.168.1.100' and asserts '192.168.1.100' in output and 'localhost' not in output — passes.

acceptanceCriteria.text: When `proc.stdout` yields no URL-matching line, the command exits non-zero and stdout is empty; verified by the new timeout test (R8)
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: S13_UrlFlag.test_url_flag_timeout_exits_nonzero_no_stdout passes. dhtplay:228-233: when _find_stream_url returns None, prints error to stderr and returns 3. Test patches _find_stream_url to return None, asserts rc != 0 and out.getvalue() == ''.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The `--url` Popen call does not include `--quiet` in its argument list; verified by the replacement S13 test (R7)
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: S13_UrlFlag.test_url_flag_no_quiet passes. dhtplay:219: wt_cmd_http = [wt, magnet, '-p', str(args.port), '--keep-seeding'] — no --quiet present. Test captures all Popen cmd args and asserts '--quiet' not in captured_args.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `_find_stream_url` is called with the subprocess handle and `args.port`; no new URL-scanning logic is introduced; verified by reading `dhtplay` lines 221–270 after the change
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: dhtplay:227: raw_url = _find_stream_url(proc, args.port, timeout=30). grep shows exactly two lines: line 116 (definition) and line 227 (call site inside if args.url: block). No new URL-scanning logic introduced.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `grep -n "urlopen" dhtplay` returns no results — the HTTP polling loop is fully removed
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: grep -n 'urlopen' dhtplay returns exit code 1 with no output. The polling loop and urllib imports are fully removed.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `python3 test_dhtplay.py` exits 0 with all scenarios passing after the change
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: python3 test_dhtplay.py: 44/44 passed — ALL PASS. Exit code 0.

decisions: Use existing _find_stream_url function unchanged — wire it in, don't rewrite it
Remove --quiet from the webtorrent command and pipe stdout so the URL announcement reaches _find_stream_url
Patch _find_stream_url in timeout test rather than using iter([]) with the real function to avoid blocking for the full 30s timeout

changeAnnotations.note: Rewrites the --url branch: removes HTTP polling loop, adds stdout=PIPE+text=True to Popen, calls _find_stream_url to read the real streaming URL from webtorrent stdout, applies LAN IP substitution, handles timeout/exit error cases

Updates S13-S19 to use proc_mock.stdout iterators instead of urlopen mocks; adds test_url_flag_no_quiet and test_url_flag_timeout_exits_nonzero_no_stdout; renames test_url_flag_prints_http_url_with_infohash to test_url_flag_prints_http_url_with_lan_ip
